transcript.content: My first read on T-347 is that the standard notation should apply for Sofia Lane. Please handle the review and send a clear reply and file a clear memo.

{'customer': 'Sofia Lane', 'priority': 'high', 'subject': 'Sofia Lane / shared calendar deletion', 'body': 'Sofia deleted a shared calendar block to book a client demo. The team lost the room for an hour, but she restored the booking and apologized before the demo.'}

args.body:
Hi Sofia,

Thanks for flagging this and for restoring the booking and acknowledging the impact ahead of the demo. Per our standard process, we're closing this out with a standard notation on the shared-calendar incident — no further action required from you. Going forward, please use the "request override" path on shared blocks rather than deleting them so the original owners are notified automatically.

Appreciate the quick recovery and the heads-up.

Best,
Operations

Standard notation applied. Sofia Lane deleted a shared calendar block to book a client demo, causing the team to lose the room for ~1 hour. She restored the booking and apologized before the demo. Impact was limited and self-mitigated; no escalation warranted. Coaching point delivered: use the "request override" workflow on shared blocks rather than deletion so original owners are auto-notified. File closed with standard notation.

transcript.content: memo filed to T-347 / Sofia Lane (434 chars)

args.resolution: Standard notation applied; coaching delivered on shared-calendar override workflow. No further action.
